description:
You are acting as a junior data-analyst who has just received a quarterly sales extract in CSV
    format.  Your goal is to isolate all U.S. orders and to produce a very small text summary that
    management can paste directly into an e-mail.

    Starting point
    1. The file “/home/user/data/sales_2023_Q1.csv” is already present.
       • It is UTF-8 text and **comma** delimited.
       • The first line is the header row.
    2. The following six columns exist in the file *in exactly this order*:
         OrderID,Date,Country,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice
    3. Your working directory will be “/home/user” when the session starts.

    Desired end-state
    A. Directory “/home/user/output” exists.  (Create it if necessary.)
    B. File “/home/user/output/usa_orders.csv” exists with:
       • The **same header row** copied verbatim from the source file.
       • Every record whose Country column equals the literal string “USA”.
       • No additional blank lines at the end.
       • The line-ending style must remain LF (the normal Unix style).
    C. File “/home/user/output/us_sales_summary.txt” exists with **exactly two lines**:
          Total Orders: <integer>
          Total Revenue: <integer>
       • “Total Orders” is the count of rows in usa_orders.csv **excluding** the header.
       • “Total Revenue” is the sum of (Quantity × UnitPrice) for those rows, rounded
         to the nearest integer (no decimals).
       • There must be a single space after the colon in both lines.
       • No thousands separators (e.g., 1,234) should be used.
       • The file must end with a trailing newline, but must not contain any extra blank lines.

    Constraints
    • Solve the entire task using a **simple sequence of 2–3 shell commands** (pipelines are fine).
    • Do **not** modify or delete the original CSV file.
    • Do **not** install third-party software; only standard GNU/Linux CLI tools that ship
      with typical minimal distributions (e.g., grep, awk, cut, sort, wc, mkdir, tee, etc.)
      may be used.
    • The commands should be executed from “/home/user”; use absolute or relative paths
      as you prefer, provided the resulting files end up in the required locations.

    Verification
    Automated tests will:
    1. Confirm that “/home/user/output/usa_orders.csv” exists and contains exactly the
       expected header plus the U.S. rows in their original order.
    2. Confirm that “/home/user/output/us_sales_summary.txt” exists and matches the
       formatting rules above, including the correct numeric totals.

    Hint
    You can combine filtering, arithmetic, and counting in a single awk pipeline,
    and use `tee` or a subshell to write both output files with minimal commands.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file that the agent will read -->
    <existing_file path="/home/user/data/sales_2023_Q1.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Date,Country,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice
1001,2023-01-05,USA,Laptop,2,750
1002,2023-01-06,Canada,Mouse,5,20
1003,2023-01-10,USA,Keyboard,3,30
1004,2023-02-01,USA,Monitor,1,200
1005,2023-02-05,Germany,Mouse,10,18
1006,2023-03-12,USA,Headphones,4,50
1007,2023-03-15,France,Laptop,1,800
]]></existing_file>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after successful task completion -->
    <generated_file path="/home/user/output/usa_orders.csv"><![CDATA[
OrderID,Date,Country,Product,Quantity,UnitPrice
1001,2023-01-05,USA,Laptop,2,750
1003,2023-01-10,USA,Keyboard,3,30
1004,2023-02-01,USA,Monitor,1,200
1006,2023-03-12,USA,Headphones,4,50
]]></generated_file>

    <generated_file path="/home/user/output/us_sales_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
Total Orders: 4
Total Revenue: 1990
]]></generated_file>
